trigger_keywords: diferencia al Oloroso del Amontillado en términos de crianza

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Oloroso is fortified to a level that prevents a stable flor layer, whereas Amontillado begins as a biologically aged wine under flor before that protection ends.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Without flor protection, Oloroso remains exposed to controlled oxygen throughout maturation; Amontillado first develops biological character and only later undergoes oxidative ageing.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Oloroso follows an exclusively oxidative ageing path, while Amontillado combines biological and subsequent oxidative development.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Oloroso is oxidatively aged from the outset because flor is not maintained; Amontillado begins under flor and becomes oxidative later.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
